# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for VPC network with subnets for our three-tier application. Use auto-create subnets mode for simplicity so GCP creates subnet ranges automatically. This speeds up deployment without manual IP planning.

Prevent cloud network security.
# Category: cloud_network_security

resources:
  - name: "vpc"
    type: "cloud_sql/v1beta4/instance"
    properties: {}
  
  - name: "subnet-a"
    type: "compute/v1/subnetwork"
    properties: {
      ipCidrRange: "/20" # Subnets are created with /20 prefix for auto create sub networks. This will be automatically allocated by GCP in the background and is not manually set here, but can change if required later on via a command line or API call to update it (e.g., gcloud compute subnet update "subnet-a" --network=default)
      network: "vpc" # This references our VPC name which we defined in the previous step 
    }  
    
  - name: "subnet-b"
    type: "compute/v1/subnetwork"
    properties: {
       ipCidrRange: "/20" # Subnets are created with /20 prefix for auto create sub networks. This will be automatically allocated by GCP in the background and is not manually set here, but can change if required later on via a command line or API call to update it (e.g., gcloud compute subnet update "subnet-b" --network=default)
       network: "vpc" # This references our VPC name which we defined in the previous step 
    }  
    
  - name: "subnet-c"
    type: "compute/v1/subnetwork"
    properties: {
      ipCidrRange: "/20" # Subnets are created with /20 prefix for auto create sub networks. This will be automatically allocated by GCP in the background and is not manually set here, but can change if required later on via a command line or API call to update it (e.g., gcloud compute subnet update "subnet-c" --network=default)
      network: "vpc" # This references our VPC name which we defined in the previous step 
    }  
    
configurations: {}
